subject: [DMaaP] Make DMaaP Service Mesh compatible
commit_message:
[DMaaP] Make DMaaP Service Mesh compatible

Normal behaviour shouldn't change (https, use of AAF, same
configuration, ...).

Change configuration on DMaaP in order to:
 * Prefer use of (non headless) services
 * Name the port with their protocol
 * allow to disable https (and enable http)
 * disable use of AAF when https is disabled

Change-Id: Icaabaf8755e685600c13b56ae6cfbcfcd3e986f5
Issue-ID: OOM-2233
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 5:

__Should__ work without service mesh.
add global.serviceMesh.enabled, global.serviceMesh.tls and global.serviceMesh.provider to istio and it __should__ work also on istio (if you have used https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/99478 as postgres wouldn't work).
I have sometimes some glitches on kakfa and dmaap-bc so you have to check the logs
@dominic, making crash dmaap-bc instead of "not really crashing" would be better IMHO...

Patch Set 9:

(2 comments)

Guys,
Some « general » comments on this merge request:

* it will not work if this patch is not applied (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/99478) as Bus Controller now uses pgpool as Postgres entry point, which is buggy without the patch. I don’t know how to make a Merge request with two ancestors so I had to choose one.
* the default behavior is to work as today (so no service mesh but TLS and AAF). I’ve tested it and it seems to be the case. 
* I tried not to change too much the code but when changes had to be big, I’ve used the new templates. There will be another merge request later which will do the job for the rest (as DMaaP is the cornerstone of ONAP, I prefer to start with it, sorry guys ;-) )
* the main goal for me before I go back to work was to have something working without and with service mesh (istio only) so Lucjan can work on the istio ingress part if he wants. 

So there’s no hurry for this patch but feel free to review/comment.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 9:

Comments/Questions:

There are some changes that may not at all related to Service Mesh changes(Some of them are trivial such as changing the port names and service name etc, but some will definitely create issues such as changing the Kafka endpoints in MsgRtrApi.properties). Please try to avoid them as much as possible

In order to stop using AAF, more changes are required both in the Helm charts( disable the SASL related properties in server.properties) and Docker images. Is there any env variable based on which I can add and remove the AAF related code in Docker images

Is there any documentation on what you are trying to do and its impact on the each application. What is the timeline for implementing this change (Looks like we need some changes in Docker images and as we are moving to the Confluent Platform, so we are completely rewriting the Kafka and Zookeeper images and  Helm charts, I have to include these change in the new charts)

Patch Set 9:

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> Comments/Questions:
> 
> There are some changes that may not at all related to Service Mesh changes(Some of them are trivial such as changing the port names and service name etc, but some will definitely create issues such as changing the Kafka endpoints in MsgRtrApi.properties). Please try to avoid them as much as possible

Changing the port names is mandatory in order for service mesh to know the type of traffic. I'm not sure I changed the name of a service but I definitely tried to normalize service creation.

> 
> In order to stop using AAF, more changes are required both in the Helm charts( disable the SASL related properties in server.properties) and Docker images. Is there any env variable based on which I can add and remove the AAF related code in Docker images

There are no such env variable in order to do that but it's fairly trivial to add. So please give me the name you've decided and I'll adapt the charts

> 
> Is there any documentation on what you are trying to do and its impact on the each application. What is the timeline for implementing this change (Looks like we need some changes in Docker images and as we are moving to the Confluent Platform, so we are completely rewriting the Kafka and Zookeeper images and  Helm charts, I have to include these change in the new charts)

Plan is to have a demo of instantiation of a service during Prague DDF. I can help on the helm chart (and docker also i believe) if you want

Patch Set 11:

@Sylvain
OMG this instruction looks like a nightmare...
Personally I'd just do:
$ git fetch origin
$ git checkout origin/master
$ git fetch "https://gerrit.onap.org/r/oom" refs/changes/56/99856/11 && git cherry-pick FETCH_HEAD
# Above command is copy-paste from the download menu on the right hand side of this gerrit review
$ git gui
and then you just go to commit-> amend last commit
not you can add or remove anything that you want from this commit so you can split it to several smaller changes and the just do:
$ git rebase -i origin/master 
to reorder them
Abandoned
